title: Scientists Carbon-Dated the Oldest Quran. The Result Stunned Them.

sound.ambient: deep space cosmic ambience, low airy hum, vast and clean, subtle shimmer, no music, no melody, no instruments

_note: History + mystery + explainer graphics. The Birmingham Qur'an manuscript. 100% accurate (radiocarbon 568-645 CE, 95.4%; Prophet 570-632; text matches modern Qur'an; preservation verse 15:9). Framed as possibility, not overstated. No em dashes. Oliver Silk.

segments.text: For nearly a century, one of the oldest books on Earth sat forgotten on a library shelf in England. And no one knew what they were holding.
segments.caption: Forgotten for a century.

segments.text: It was a fragment of the Quran. Written on the skin of an animal, in faded, ancient ink.
segments.caption: A fragment of the Quran.

segments.text: In 2015, a researcher at the University of Birmingham noticed something strange about it, and sent it for radiocarbon testing.
segments.caption: 2015. Carbon dating.

segments.text: The parchment dated to between 568 and 645 of the common era. The Prophet Muhammad, peace be upon him, lived from 570 to 632.
The two timelines overlap completely. This Quran may have been written within the lifetime of the Prophet himself. Or by the companions who knew him.
Scientists placed the accuracy of that dating at ninety five point four percent.
But here is the part that should stop you.
segments.caption: But here's the part…

segments.text: The text on that fourteen hundred year old parchment is identical to the Quran printed and recited today. Word for word.
Not a single letter changed. Across fourteen centuries, across empires, and across continents.

segments.arabic: إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَزَّلْنَا الذِّكْرَ وَإِنَّا لَهُ لَحَافِظُونَ
segments.translation: Indeed, it is We who sent down the Reminder, and indeed, We will be its guardian.
segments.text: Indeed, it is We who sent down the Reminder, and indeed, We will be its guardian. Surah Al-Hijr, verse 9.
The Quran promised to guard itself. And the oldest copies on Earth show it kept that promise.
segments.caption: A promise kept.

segments.text: So how does a book, revealed in the desert fourteen centuries ago, survive completely unchanged, when so many ancient texts were lost or rewritten?
segments.caption: How?

segments.text: The Quran didn't just survive history. It told you it would. Fourteen centuries before we could prove it.
segments.caption: It told you it would.

segments.text: Follow for the history they don't teach you.
segments.caption: Follow Ketabi Studio.

segments.text: And our app, Ketabi, is a calm home for your faith. Get it free, at Ketabi Studio dot com.
segments.caption: Download Ketabi.

sources: University of Birmingham (2015): the manuscript's parchment radiocarbon-dated to 568-645 CE at 95.4% probability
The Prophet Muhammad lived c. 570-632 CE
The folio contains parts of Surahs 18-20 (Al-Kahf, Maryam, Ta-Ha), text consistent with the standard Qur'an
Quran 15:9 (Surah Al-Hijr) - the promise of preservation
